title: Daily Rosary Companion — Marian Citations for Each Mystery

mysteries.title: The Annunciation
mysteries.scripture: **Scripture** (Luke 1:38, Greek): *εἶπεν δὲ Μαριάμ· ἰδοὺ ἡ δούλη Κυρίου· γένοιτό μοι κατὰ τὸ ῥῆμά σου.* **English**: "And Mary said: Behold the handmaid of the Lord; be it done to me according to thy word."
mysteries.fruit: humility
mysteries.intention: for the grace to say yes to God in the small matters of

mysteries.title: The Visitation
mysteries.scripture: **Scripture** (Luke 1:42-43): "And [Elizabeth] cried out with a loud voice, and said: Blessed art thou among women, and blessed is the fruit of thy womb. And whence is this to me, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?"
mysteries.fruit: charity in service to neighbor
mysteries.intention: for those who carry heavy burdens, that the Mediatrix

mysteries.title: The Nativity of Our Lord
mysteries.scripture: **Scripture** (Luke 2:7): "And she brought forth her firstborn son, and wrapped him up in swaddling clothes, and laid him in a manger."
mysteries.fruit: poverty of spirit
mysteries.intention: for mothers laboring tonight, that the Bethlehem Mother

mysteries.title: The Presentation of Our Lord
mysteries.scripture: **Scripture** (Luke 2:34-35): "And Simeon blessed them, and said to Mary his mother: Behold this child is set for the fall, and for the resurrection of many in Israel, and for a sign which shall be contradicted; and thy own soul a sword shall pierce, that, out of many hearts, thoughts may be revealed."
mysteries.fruit: obedience and joyful acceptance of God's will
mysteries.intention: for parents whose children carry hidden swords today

mysteries.title: The Finding of Our Lord in the Temple
mysteries.scripture: **Scripture** (Luke 2:51): "And he went down with them, and came to Nazareth, and was subject to them. And his mother kept all these words in her heart."
mysteries.fruit: the gift of pondering Scripture in the heart
mysteries.intention: for those who have lost Christ in the present moment,

mysteries.scripture: **Scripture** (Matthew 3:17): "And behold a voice from heaven, saying: This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased."
mysteries.fruit: openness to the gifts of the Holy Spirit
mysteries.intention: for the renewal of our baptismal promises through her

mysteries.title: The Wedding at Cana
mysteries.reference: John 2:1-11) — THE MARIAN MYSTERY OF MEDIATION
mysteries.scripture: **Scripture** (John 2:5, Greek): *λέγει ἡ μήτηρ αὐτοῦ τοῖς διακόνοις· ὅ τι ἂν λέγῃ ὑμῖν, ποιήσατε.* **English**: "His mother saith to the waiters: **Whatsoever he shall say to you, do ye.**"
mysteries.fruit: to Jesus through Mary
mysteries.intention: for the petition you have not yet dared to bring; speak

mysteries.fruit: desire for holiness, transfiguration in Christ

mysteries.scripture: **Scripture** (Luke 22:19): "Hoc est corpus meum, quod pro vobis datur." ("This is my body, which is given for you.")
mysteries.fruit: Eucharistic devotion, sacramental adoration

mysteries.title: The Scourging at the Pillar
mysteries.scripture: **Scripture** (Isaiah 53:5): "But he was wounded for our iniquities, he was bruised for our sins: the chastisement of our peace was upon him, and by his bruises we are healed."
mysteries.fruit: mortification, purity, self-control

mysteries.title: The Crowning with Thorns
mysteries.scripture: **Scripture** (John 19:5): "Jesus therefore came forth, bearing the crown of thorns and the purple garment. And he saith to them: Behold the man." (*Ecce homo.*)
mysteries.fruit: moral courage, contempt of the world
mysteries.intention: for those mocked, slandered, or humiliated; for the

mysteries.title: The Carrying of the Cross
mysteries.scripture: **Scripture** (John 19:17): "And bearing his own cross, he went forth to that place which is called Calvary, but in Hebrew Golgotha."
mysteries.fruit: patience, perseverance under affliction

mysteries.title: The Crucifixion and Death of Our Lord
mysteries.scripture: **Scripture** (John 19:25-27, Greek): *Εἱστήκεισαν δὲ παρὰ τῷ σταυρῷ τοῦ Ἰησοῦ ἡ μήτηρ αὐτοῦ... Ἰησοῦς... λέγει τῇ μητρί· γύναι, ἴδε ὁ υἱός σου. εἶτα λέγει τῷ μαθητῇ· ἴδε ἡ μήτηρ σου.* **English**: "Now there stood by the cross of Jesus, his mother... When Jesus therefore had seen his mother and the disciple standing whom he loved, he saith to his mother: **Woman, behold thy son.** After that, he saith to the disciple: **Behold thy mother.**"
mysteries.fruit: final perseverance, the gift of dying in Christ
mysteries.intention: for those who will die today without a mother; that

mysteries.title: The Resurrection of Our Lord
mysteries.scripture: **Scripture** (Romans 6:9): "Christ rising again from the dead, dieth now no more, death shall no more have dominion over him."
mysteries.fruit: faith, hope of bodily resurrection

mysteries.fruit: love of God, gifts of the Holy Spirit, missionary zeal

mysteries.title: The Coronation of the Blessed Virgin Mary
